heading: Authority to maintain existing areas of conformity between Civil Service and Central Intelligence Agency Retirement and Disability Systems
label: 2141 Authority to maintain existing areas of conformity between Civil Service and Central Intelligence Agency Retirement and Disability Systems

Open Enrollment Season for Participants in the Central Intelligence Agency Retirement and Disability SystemThe Director to provide an open enrollment period for employee participants in the Central Intelligence Agency Retirement and Disability System to elect the Federal Employees’ Retirement System, see Ex. Ord. No. 13105, § 2, , 63 F.R. 60201, set out as a note under , Foreign Relations and Intercourse.Nov. 2, 1998section 4067 of Title 22

Ex. Ord. No. 13236. Waiver of Dual Compensation ProvisionsEx. Ord. No. 13236, , 66 F.R. 59671, provided:Nov. 27, 2001By the authority vested in me as President by the Constitution and the laws of the United States of America, including section 292 of the Central Intelligence Agency Retirement Act of 1964 [Central Intelligence Agency Retirement Act], as amended (), and in order to conform the Central Intelligence Agency Retirement and Disability System to the Civil Service Retirement and Disability System, it is hereby ordered as follows:50 U.S.C. 21411. The Director of Central Intelligence may waive the application of the dual compensation reduction provisions of sections 271 and 273 of the Central Intelligence Agency Retirement Act ( and 2113) for an employee serving on a temporary basis, but only if, and for so long as, the authority is necessary due to an emergency involving a direct threat to life or property or other unusual circumstances. Employees who receive both salary and annuity pursuant to this authority may not earn additional retirement benefits during this period of employment. This authority may be delegated as appropriate.Section50 U.S.C. 2111. 2. Nothing contained in this order is intended to create, nor does it create, any right, benefit, or privilege, substantive or procedural, enforceable at law by a party against the United States, its agencies, officers, employees, or any other person.SecGeorge W. Bush.

children.heading: Conformity to CSRS by Executive order
children.label: 1 Conformity to CSRS by Executive order
A
children.text: amends subchapter III of chapter 83 of title 5 and is applicable to civil service employees generally; or

children.label: B
children.text: otherwise affects current or former participants in the Civil Service Retirement and Disability System, or their survivors.

children.heading: Extension to CIARDS
children.label: 2 Extension to CIARDS
children.text: Any such order shall extend such provision of law so that it applies in like manner with respect to such Central Intelligence Agency Retirement and Disability System participants, former participants, or survivors.

children.heading: Legal status
children.label: 3 Legal status
children.text: Any such order shall have the force and effect of law.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: 4 Effective date
children.text: Any such order may be given retroactive effect to a date not earlier than the effective date of the corresponding provision of law applicable to employees under the Civil Service Retirement System.
provisions of law enacted before the effective date of the Executive order; and
any prior provision of an Executive order issued under this section.
